findings.evidence: Lines 166 and 171 carry `@[simp]`; line 179 does not.
findings.issue: `mapValue_comp` is missing `@[simp]`, inconsistent with `mapValue_apply` (line 166) and `mapValue_id` (line 171). It is a normal-form compositionality lemma of the same kind.

findings.evidence: `private noncomputable def antipodeComp` at line 87; `private lemma toLinearMap_antipodeComp` at line 95. Mathlib TODO at Mathlib/RingTheory/HopfAlgebra/Basic.lean:33 is not addressed by any public definition in this PR.
findings.fix: Either expose the result (e.g. `noncomputable def antipodeAlgHom : H →ₐ[R] H` under `[CommSemiring H] [Algebra R H] [HopfAlgebra R H]`) or correct the description.
findings.issue: The PR description claims to discharge the Mathlib TODO 'if `A` is commutative then `antipode` is an algebra homomorphism' as `HopfAlgebra.antipodeAlgHom`, but the code only adds a `private` `AlgHom.antipodeComp` and never exposes the antipode as an `R`-algebra homomorphism `H →ₐ[R] H`.

findings.evidence: `AlgHom.ofLinearMap` is a regular def (Mathlib/Algebra/Algebra/Hom.lean:305). All proof fields use `by simp`/`by rw` of definitional lemmas; `toConv (φ.comp f.ofConv)` is a composition of computable functions.
findings.fix: Drop `noncomputable` from these definitions; if any are not computable in your environment, isolate the specific noncomputable part.
findings.issue: `antipodeComp` (87), `Inst : Inv` (101), `instGroup` (140), `mapValue` (155), and `instCommGroup` (193) are all `noncomputable` despite being computable.

summary: The convolution group structure is correctly added, but the public API has gaps: `mapValue_comp` lacks `@[simp]`, the description's claimed `HopfAlgebra.antipodeAlgHom` is not actually exposed (the helper is `private`), and `noncomputable` is over-applied to computable definitions.
